results.title: India Launches Smart Warehousing System to Modernise Foodgrain Storage
results.description: The Smart Warehousing System integrates cutting-edge technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), the Internet of Things (IoT), automation, and advanced analytics. These features will help streamline warehouse operations, improve governance, and enhance visibility across storage facilities. results.title: India News | Kalaburagi NEET Exam: Police, CRPF Deployment, CCTV and Biometric Checks in Place
results.description: Get latest articles and stories on India at LatestLY. Kalaburagi City Police Commissioner Sharanappa S D said elaborate security arrangements, including police and CRPF deployment, CCTV cameras, jammers and biometric verification, have been put in place for the NEET examination. Around 600 students will appear for the exam across 25 blocks, with entry scheduled between 11:00 AM and 1:30 PM after frisking and verification, and the exam beginning at 2:00 PM.

results.title: Ailsa Lister reprimanded for ICC Code of Conduct breach at Women’s T20 World Cup
results.description: Ailsa Lister receives an official ICC reprimand for breaching Level 1 of the Code of Conduct during Scotland’s Women’s T20 World Cup clash against the West Indies.

results.title: Weather tomorrow: IMD warns of very heavy rain, thunderstorms across several states; heatwave alert in East UP, Vidarbha
results.description: Heavy to very heavy rainfall is predicted for Northeast India, including Assam and Meghalaya, with thunderstorms and gusty winds expected across many states. Isolated heatwave conditions are also forecast for parts of East Uttar Pradesh and Vidarbha. Residents in affected areas should be aware of potential localized flooding and disruptions due to severe weather.

results.title: RVNL Wins ₹2,977 Crore NMDC Contract, Major Vizag Infrastructure Project To Boost Order Book
results.description: Rail Vikas Nigam Limited (RVNL) has secured a Rs 2,977 crore contract from NMDC to build a 10 MTPA buffer stockpile and blending yard in Vizag. The 42-month project strengthens RVNL’s infrastructure portfolio and improves long-term revenue visibility.

Meloni had earlier called Trump a liar on Friday after he claimed she had "begged" him for a photograph with her during this...

results.title: Delhi Shocker: Woman Killed, Husband Critically Injured in Stabbing After Parking Dispute Escalates in Bindapur
results.description: A 32-year-old woman was killed and her husband sustained critical injuries after an alleged stabbing following a dispute over motorcycle parking in southwest Delhi's Bindapur area. Police have arrested one accused and apprehended a juvenile in connection with the incident, which occurred on Saturday afternoon.

results.title: Dream project of bullet train will be operational in 2027: Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw
results.description: Union Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw on Sunday said that India’s dream bullet train project will become operational in 2027 and will be opened section by section. Speaking on the sidelines of the 12th annual International Day of Yoga in Bikaner, Vaishnaw said, “The dream project of the bullet train will be operational in 2027 and will open section by section. This dream will be fulfilled in 2027.” The Mumbai–Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail Corridor, India’s first bullet train project, is currently under construction. While work on the corridor has progressed in phases, the government has reiterated its commitment to completing and operationalising the project in 2027. India’s high-speed rail ambitions, however, extend far beyond the Mumbai–Ahmedabad route. As part of its long-term vision to build a nationwide bullet train network, the government has already identified seven additional high-speed rail corridors. #WATCH | Bikaner | Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw says, "The dream project of the bullet train will be operational in 2027 and will open section by section. This dream will be fulfilled in 2027" pic.twitter.com/wFAy0ydIpz — ANI (@ANI) June 21, 2026 The proposed routes include Mumbai -Pune, Pune-Hyderabad, Hyderabad- Bengaluru , Hyderabad-Chennai, Chennai-Bengaluru, Delhi -Varanasi and Varanasi-Siliguri. The expansion plans underline the government's broader strategy of developing a modern, high-speed rail network across India, with the Mumbai-Ahmedabad Bullet Train project serving as the foundation for future corridors.
